trivia_bullets.text: Brilliant Spectrum is a Converge sorcery from Battle for Zendikar (October 2, 2015). The depicted figure — elf-eared, bare-chested, arms outstretched in rainbow light — is unnamed in oracle text and flavor text. Bolbuss-pattern check applied: no named Zendikar elf can be attributed from canonical sources.

Converge rewards spending mana of multiple colors, scaling effect by color count (max 5). Brilliant Spectrum draws X cards equal to colors spent, then discards two. At full five-color convergence, it nets three cards. Mark Rosewater's Storm Scale article rated Converge 'Unpopular' in market research — lowest or near-lowest rated BFZ mechanic — with 'Small' design space due to scaling-effect constraints.
sources: magic.wizards.com: Storm Scale: Zendikar and Battle for Zendikar (2016)

trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: 'There are more sights on Zendikar than the eyes can see.' Unattributed aphorism. Reflects Zendikar's identity as an Adventure World — a plane defined by its spectacular and dangerous geography, a design mandate carried through both Zendikar blocks.

EDHREC rank 25,792 and market price $0.02 reflect near-zero competitive uptake. In practice, Converge requires multicolor mana support to draw more than one or two cards, and the mandatory discard-two clause limits the ceiling even at full convergence. Outclassed by Harmonize, Drawn from Dreams, or Strategic Planning for most Commander slots.
